text.title: portfolio-strategy Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for portfolio-strategy as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: The same person plays product-PM and portfolio-PM without a written allocation memo.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The framework cannot resolve a conflict of interest if the same human owns both hats. A written memo makes trade-offs visible to others who can catch drift.

failure-mode.description: Each product-PM optimizes their P&L for more H1, and the aggregate portfolio drifts to 95% H1 with no transformational bets.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Local incentives aggregate to a portfolio failure. Counter: portfolio-PM owns the H3 budget separately from product budgets and cannot be lobbied out of it.

failure-mode.description: One human running 6+ products attempts a real 70/20/10 split but exploration time collapses to zero under operational load.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Document the collapse honestly instead of pretending the ratio is real. A false ratio in the portfolio memo misleads future agents and reviews.

failure-mode.description: Portfolio math does not score work that benefits multiple products simultaneously, so shared platform work is under-funded.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Product PMs resist sharing their roadmap slot to fund shared infra. The portfolio-PM must account for cross-product leverage explicitly, not assume product PMs will volunteer it.

failure-mode.description: Cross-product trade-offs negotiated 1:1 between product PMs rather than in a single portfolio memo signed by the portfolio-PM.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bilateral deals leak budget and never balance. Each PM "wins" locally; the portfolio loses in aggregate.

failure-mode.description: Agent conflates "PM owns more products" with "PM is more strategic" and auto-tags multi-product PMs as portfolio-PMs regardless of time allocation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The decision frame shift from feature-level to cross-product investment is qualitative. Force the classifier to use a time_allocation field, not a product count.

failure-mode.description: Agent recommends splitting the difference when two PMs disagree, hiding the actual trade-off.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Compromise memos avoid the hard call. Constrain the rebalancer to pick a side with a rationale; do not average positions.
